---
title: "FinalProject(6)"
author: "Mizuki Shitomi (905863786)"
date: "2025-12-07"
output: pdf_document
---

```{r}
set.seed(87)
train <- read.csv("~/Documents/UCLA/YEAR 4/STATS 101C/FINAL PROJECT/train_imputed.csv")
test  <- read.csv("~/Documents/UCLA/YEAR 4/STATS 101C/FINAL PROJECT/test_imputed.csv")
train$Cancer <- factor(train$Cancer)
library(MASS)
library(glmnet)
library(caret)   # <-- add caret
```


# LDA + Scaling/Centering + Dropping Cor Pred

```{r}
# Drop clearly uninformative / "fun" variables

drop_vars <- c(
"favorite_color",
"favorite_cuisine",
"phone_brand",
"preferred_shoe_type",
"music_genre",
"pets",
"uses_smartwatch"
)

# Identify numeric columns
num_cols <- sapply(train, is.numeric)

# Correlation matrix
corr_mat <- cor(train[, num_cols])

# Find highly correlated predictors
high_corr <- findCorrelation(corr_mat, cutoff = 0.90)

# Remove them
if (length(high_corr) > 0) {
  remove_names <- names(train[, num_cols])[high_corr]
  train <- train[, !(names(train) %in% remove_names)]
  test  <- test[,  !(names(test)  %in% remove_names)]
}

train <- train[ , !(names(train) %in% drop_vars)]
test  <- test[  , !(names(test)  %in% drop_vars)]

# 10-fold cross-validation

ctrl <- trainControl(
method = "cv",
number = 10
)

# LDA model with ONE interaction term added

# (this is the only real change vs your original model)

lda_fit <- train(
  Cancer ~ .,
  data      = train,
  method    = "lda",
  trControl = ctrl,
  preProcess = c("center", "scale")   # <-- SMALL CHANGE, BIG IMPACT
)

lda_fit

# Training predictions and accuracy

train_pred     <- predict(lda_fit, newdata = train)
train_accuracy <- mean(train_pred == train$Cancer)
train_accuracy

# Test predictions for Kaggle submission

test_pred <- predict(lda_fit, newdata = test)

pred <- data.frame(
ID     = 1:nrow(test),
Cancer = factor(test_pred, levels = c("Benign", "Malignant"))
)

write.csv(pred, "~/Documents/UCLA/YEAR 4/STATS 101C/FINAL PROJECT/submission_lda_scaling_drop_cor.csv", row.names = FALSE)
```

# Ridge Regression 

```{r}
# Drop clearly uninformative / "fun" variables

drop_vars <- c(
"favorite_color",
"favorite_cuisine",
"phone_brand",
"preferred_shoe_type",
"music_genre",
"pets",
"uses_smartwatch"
)

# Identify numeric columns
num_cols <- sapply(train, is.numeric)

# Correlation matrix
corr_mat <- cor(train[, num_cols])

# Find highly correlated predictors
high_corr <- findCorrelation(corr_mat, cutoff = 0.90)

# Remove them
if (length(high_corr) > 0) {
  remove_names <- names(train[, num_cols])[high_corr]
  train <- train[, !(names(train) %in% remove_names)]
  test  <- test[,  !(names(test)  %in% remove_names)]
}

train <- train[ , !(names(train) %in% drop_vars)]
test  <- test[  , !(names(test)  %in% drop_vars)]

# 10-fold cross-validation

ctrl <- trainControl(
method = "cv",
number = 10
)

# LDA model with ONE interaction term added

# (this is the only real change vs your original model)

# glmnet logistic regression (ridge)
glmnet_fit <- train(
  Cancer ~ .,
  data       = train,
  method     = "glmnet",
  trControl  = ctrl,
  preProcess = c("center", "scale"),   # keep this, it helps a lot
  tuneGrid   = expand.grid(
    alpha = 0,                         # ridge penalty
    lambda = seq(0.0001, 1, length = 50)
  )
)
glmnet_fit

# Training predictions and accuracy

train_pred     <- predict(glmnet_fit, newdata = train)
train_accuracy <- mean(train_pred == train$Cancer)
train_accuracy

# Test predictions for Kaggle submission

test_pred <- predict(glmnet_fit, newdata = test)

pred <- data.frame(
ID     = 1:nrow(test),
Cancer = factor(test_pred, levels = c("Benign", "Malignant"))
)

write.csv(pred, "~/Documents/UCLA/YEAR 4/STATS 101C/FINAL PROJECT/submission_glmnet_ridge.csv", row.names = FALSE)
```
